title: GeoLocator Data Package: Barred Warblers from Czechia

contributors.title: Adamik, Peter
contributors.organization: Palacký University Olomouc, Museum of Natural History

description:
This Geolocator Data Package contains the analyzed data (using GeoPressureR) for two Barred Warblers tracked from Czechia. This data has been published here: Migration and daily flight activity patterns in the barred warbler Curruca nisoria over the annual cycle.

If you would like to use the data, please contact Joanna Wong (joanna.wong@vogelwarte.ch)
bibliographicCitation:
Wong J, Adamik P, Bažant M, Hahn S (2025). “GeoLocator Data Package: Barred Warblers
from Czechia.” doi:10.5281/zenodo.17367319
<https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17367319>.

title: GeoLocator Data Package: Eurasian Nightjar from Mongolia

contributors.title: Lathouwers, Michiel
contributors.organization: Research Institute for Nature and Forest

description:
This Geolocator DataPackage describe the migration routes and timing of European Nightjars breeding in Eastern Mongolia

Original code that performed the analysis following the GeoPressureTemplate format can be found at Rafnuss/MongolianNightjar

The main publication associated with this datapacakge is:

Lathouwers, M., Nussbaumer, R., Liechti, F. et al. Migration routes and timing of European Nightjars (Caprimulgus europaeus) breeding in eastern Mongolia. J Ornithol (2022). https://doi.org/10.1007/s10336-022-02000-4

Cite this datapackage with:

Lathouwers M, Nussbaumer R, Kowalczyk C, Davaasuren B, Bayrgur S, Evens R (2025). “GeoLocator Data Package: Mongolian Nightjars.” Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.16730669

  • Spatial: Polygon and c(22.75, 112.75, 112.75, 22.75, 22.75, -33.25, -33.25, 49.75, 49.75, -33.25)
  • Temporal: "2018-07-15" to "2020-09-28"
  • Taxonomic: "Caprimulgus europaeus"
  • NumberTags:
    tags: 49
    measurements: 3
    light: 3
    pressure: 3
    activity: 3
    magnetic: 3
    paths: 3
bibliographicCitation:
Lathouwers M, Nussbaumer R, Kowalczyk C, Davaasuren B, Bayrgur S, Evens R (2025). “GeoLocator Data Package: Eurasian Nightjar from Mongolia.”
doi:10.5281/zenodo.16730669 <https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.16730669>.

title: GeoLocator Data Package: Myrtle Warbler (Anchorage, AK)
description: This GeoLocator DataPackage contains data for 6 multi-sensor geolocators (Migrate Tech BARP30Z11-DIP) deployed on Myrtle Warblers (Setophaga coronata coronata) breeding in Anchorage, Alaska. The data has been processed using the GeoPressureR package to generate migration trajectories using both atmospheric pressure and light-level data.
grants: American Ornithological Society Student Research Award
Animal Behavior Society Student Research Award
Wilson Ornithological Society Paul A. Stewart Grant
Pennsylvania State University Department of Biology, Eberly College of Science, and Huck Institute of the Life Sciences
bibliographicCitation:
Stephanie Szarmach, David Toews, Johanna Beam, Mads Moore, Benjamin Van Doren, Alan Brelsford (2025).
“GeoLocator Data Package: Myrtle Warbler (Anchorage, AK).” doi:10.5281/zenodo.17195974
<https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17195974>.

title: GeoLocator Data Package: Red-capped Robin-Chat and Mangrove Kingfisher from Kenya

contributors.title: Nussbaumer, Raphaël
contributors.organization: Swiss Ornithological Institute, A Rocha Kenya

description:
Description:
This dataset contains geolocator tracking data for two Afro-tropical migrant bird species—Red-capped Robin-Chat (Cossypha natalensis) and Mangrove Kingfisher (Halcyon senegaloides)—collected as part of a research project started in 2020 based at A Rocha Kenya, Watamu, Kenya. The project aims to uncover the migration routes, timing, and strategies of these species, which are largely unknown despite their ecological importance and vulnerability to habitat fragmentation and climate change.

How to cite:

Nussbaumer R, Kirao L, Liechti F, Jackson C (2025). “GeoLocator Data Package: Red-capped Robin-Chat and Mangrove Kingfisher from Kenya.” https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.16804931.

Feel free to reach out to use if you plan to use the data.

Data summury:

temporal: "2020-06-01" to "2024-07-25"

Number tags:
  • tags: 102
  • measurements: 23
  • light: 23
  • pressure: 19
  • activity: 20
  • magnetic: 20
  • paths: 16
Resources:
  • twilights (n=11,166)
  • staps (n=238)
  • paths (n=2,618)
  • edges (n=2,442)
  • tags (n=102)
  • observations (n=147)
  • measurements (n=7,586,081)
bibliographicCitation:
Nussbaumer R, Kirao L, Liechti F, Jackson C (2025). “GeoLocator Data Package: Red-capped
Robin-Chat and Mangrove Kingfisher from Kenya.” doi:10.5281/zenodo.16804931
<https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.16804931>.

title: GeoLocator Data Package: Siberian Rubythroat from China

contributors.title: Lei, Fumin
contributors.organization: Institute of Zoology, Chinese Academy of Sciences

contributors.title: Song, Gang
contributors.organization: Institute of Zoology, Chinese Academy of Sciences

description:
Project description

  • Year: 2020 - 2024
  • Location: Datong, Qinghai, China
  • Deployment: 31 PinPoint-10 GPS + 13 light-level geolocator (2020); 20 CAnMove logger (2021); 12 PinPoint-10 GPS (2022); 9 MigrateTech multi-sensor logger (2023)
  • Main collaborating parties: CAnMove lab, Lund University (logger supplier); Zoology Institute of China, Chinese Academy of Science, China (fieldwork arrangements); Swiss Ornithology Institute (data analysis); University of Groningen (PhD basis)
  • Other tracked sympatric species: Yellow-streaked Warbler (Phylloscopus armandii), Siberian Stonechat (Saxicola maurus przewalskii), Black-faced Bunting (Emberiza spodocephala)

How to use

Datapackage:

Zhao T, Heim W, Lei F, Song G, Helm B, Nussbaumer R (2025). “GeoLocator Data Package: Siberian Rubythroat from China.” https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17542294
Associated publication:
Zhao, T., Heim, W., Nussbaumer, R. et al. Seasonal migration patterns of Siberian Rubythroat (Calliope calliope) facing the Qinghai–Tibet Plateau. Mov Ecol 12, 54 (2024). https://doi.org/10.1186/s40462-024-00495-5

Data

  • Temporal: "2021-08-15" to "2022-06-05"
  • Taxonomic: "Calliope calliope"
  • Number of tags: 6
  • Sensors: light (only a few days), pressure and activity.
bibliographicCitation: Zhao T, Heim W, Lei F, Song G, Helm B, Nussbaumer R (2025). “GeoLocator Data Package: Siberian Rubythroat from China.” doi:10.5281/zenodo.17542294 <https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17542294>.

title: GeoLocator Data Package: South African Woodland Kingfisher

contributors.title: Nussbaumer, Raphaël
contributors.organization: Swiss Ornithological Institute, A Rocha Kenya

description:
This repository contains the raw data and the trajectory information generated with the GeoPressureR workflow, following the GeoLocator Data Package standard. The more complete code used to generate this datapackage can be found on the Github Repository Rafnuss/WoodlandKingfisher.

It contains 5 tags equipped on Woodland Kingfisher in Mogalakwena, Limpopo, South Africa between 2017-2020.

title: GeoLocator Data Package: Swainson's Warbler
description: Collaborators across the southeastern United States captured and tagged male Swainson's Warblers using multisensor geolocators (BARP50Z11-7, Migrate Technology) to study their migration ecology and migratory connectivity between eastern and western breeding populations. Using 14 tags, we implement the GeoPressureR pipeline to analyze barometric pressure data to answer our questions.
citation: Rhyne, G. (2025). GeoLocator Data Package: Swainson's Warbler [Data set]. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.14883860

title: GeoLocator Data Package: Swiss Hoopoes
description:
Temporal coverage: 2009-07-01 - 2017-08-09
Number of tags

  • tags: 1201
  • measurements: 137
  • light: 137
  • pressure: 21
  • activity: 21
  • temperature_external: 15
  • magnetic: 21
  • paths: 21
  • pressurepaths: 21

Data Collection

The data were collected as part of a long-term conservation and research project on hoopoes in the Valais, Switzerland. The birds breed in the about 600 nest boxes installed in the study area, which covers about 64 km2 along the Rhône between Martigny and Sierre and has been running at its current size since 2002. For a description of the study area and the project, see Arlettaz et al. (https://academic.oup.com/bioscience/article/60/10/835/231658) or Plard et al. (https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1002/ece3.6244).

Data Curation

The raw geolocator data have been stored on internal server at the Swiss Ornithological Instute and the technical team including a table with metadata on geolocator (device model, settings etc..)

Using GeoLocatoR, we combined (1) the raw geolocator data, (2) the information on the internal server of the vogelwarte and (3) the ringing data of the hoopoe to create this GeoLocator Data Package. During this process we followed the instruction on the GeoPressureManual using the GeoPressureTemplate Rafnuss/SwissHoopoe.

Data Analysis

The creation of the track was performed using GeoPressureR package. The code and parameters used in the model can be found at Rafnuss/SwissHoopoe.
bibliographicCitation:
Schaub M, Arlettaz R, Bauer S, van Wijk R, Liechti F, Bächler E, Capilla-Lasheras P, Nussbaumer R
(2025). “GeoLocator Data Package: Swiss Hoopoes.” doi:10.5281/zenodo.14439689
<https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.14439689>.

title: Geolocator Data Package: Trajectories of Northern Wheatears breeding in Val Piora (Switzerland) and Val Troncea (Italy)
description:
This repository contains the raw data and the trajectory information generated with the GeoPressureR workflow, following the GeoLocator Data Package standard (v0.1). The complete code used to generate this datapackage can be found at Rafnuss/Val-Piora-Wheatear.


It contains 10 tags fitted on Northern Wheatears in Val Piora, Switzerland, between 2016 and 2020 and retrieved between 2017 and 2021, and 4 tags fitted in Val Troncea, Italy, in 2019 and 2020 and retrieved in 2020 and 2021.

The main publication associated with this paper is:

Rime, Y., Nussbaumer, R., Briedis, M. et al. Multi-sensor geolocators unveil global and local movements in an Alpine-breeding long-distance migrant. Mov Ecol 11, 19 (2023). https://doi.org/10.1186/s40462-023-00381-6

title: Migration Route of Swiss Ring Ouzels with Multi-Sensor Geolocator

contributors.title: Liechti, Felix
contributors.organization: Swiss Ornithological Institute, Swiss BirdRadar Solutions

description: This GeoLocator Datapackage contains the raw data for 5 multi-sensor geolocators and 4 light-level geolocators data equipped on Alpine Ring Ouzels (Turdus torquatus alpestris) in Switzerland between 2017-2020. The data has been processed using the GeoPressureR package to produce trajectories for the 5 multi-sensor tags. Code can be found on Github Rafnuss/migration-route-of-swiss-ring-ouzels. The raw data has been used in 10.1111/jav.02860

title: GeoLocator Data Package: MIGRATLANE - Monitoring and studying birds in the North-East Atlantic Arc
description: The MIGRATLANE research programme is part of the development of offshore wind power along the French Channel-Atlantic coastline. Planning maritime developments for the energy transition must be accompanied by a good understanding of the animal species that frequent these areas in order to ensure their preservation. The French Biodiversity Agency (OFB) has therefore launched and is leading a research programme aimed at collecting data and characterising migratory flows, flight behaviour and altitudes, as well as maritime areas of importance for seabirds, land birds and bats. The programme consists of six complementary components: visual, radar, acoustic and aerial monitoring, and data analysis and modelling. Component 2, which is the subject of this data-package, focuses on telemetric monitoring of birds.
bibliographicCitation:
Paul Doniol-Valcroze, Frédéric Jiguet, Sophie de Grissac, Pierrick Bocher, Yannig Coulomb, Fanny Rey, Rose Delacroix (2025).
“GeoLocator Data Package: MIGRATLANE - Monitoring and studying birds in the North-East Atlantic Arc.”
doi:10.5281/zenodo.17631330 <https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17631330>.

title: GeoLocator Data Package: Marsh Wabler from Luxembourg
description: This project aims to investigate the migration routes and habitat use of the Marsh Warbler (Acrocephalus palustris) by deploying light-level geolocators on breeding individuals at Schlammwiss, Luxembourg. The study seeks to identify key stopover sites and wintering areas, as well as to evaluate habitat preferences along the migratory pathway. Data collected will contribute to a broader understanding of Afro-Palearctic migration systems and support conservation actions for long-distance migratory songbirds.
bibliographicCitation:
Yohannes E, Lutgen D, Nussbaumer R (2025). “GeoLocator Data Package: Marsh
Wabler from Luxembourg.” doi:10.5281/zenodo.16813863
<https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.16813863>.

title: Geolocator Data Package: European Rollers (Coracias garrulus) in Hungary, Romania and Montenegro
description: This Geolocator Data Package contains the raw and analyzed data from multi-sensor loggers retrieved from European Rollers breeding in Hungary, Romania and Montenegro. Positions and tracks were constructed using the GeoPressureR package (note: 28SI includes only raw data, and no analyzed data). This work was part of the former TrackingLeastKnownSpecies project at the Swiss Ornithological Institute.
bibliographicCitation:
Wong J, Hahn S, Kiss O, Dr. Tokody B, Daróczi S, Stumberger B (2025).

title: Geolocator Data Package: Thursh nightingale Luscinia Luscinia in South Sweden
description:
This repository contains the raw data and the trajectory information generated with the GeoPressureR workflow in thrush nightingales (Luscinia luscinia) captured near Lund, Sweden, between 2022 and 2025.

It contains data from 12 tags, 11 of which have ok pressure data, from which geoposition was possible.

This data is currently in the process of being published in several research articles.
bibliographicCitation:
Macías-Torres P, Hedenström A (2026). “Geolocator Data Package: Thursh nightingale Luscinia Luscinia in South
Sweden.” doi:10.5281/zenodo.18340650 <https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18340650>.
